item.title: 4-year-old child raped in Chattogram
item.description: Local people on Thursday held protests in Chattogram city’s Bakalia area following news of alleged rape of a four-year-old child by a youth....

item.title: Arrest of S Alam, 10 others ordered
item.description: A Chattogram court on Thursday issued warrants for five months’ civil detention for S Alam Group chairman Mohammad Saiful Alam and 10 others in a case filed over the defaulting of about Tk 80.61 crore borrowed from Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC in the......Bangladeshi Culture Course

item.title: Women, minorities sidelined: ANFREL
item.description: The Asian Network for Free Elections, in its final report on the 2026 Jatiya Sangsad elections and referendum in Bangladesh, expressed concern over low participation of women and minorities, weak campaign finance regulations, andpoliticalintimidation......

item.title: Protests erupt over child rape, murder
item.description: The government on Thursday assured a swift and exemplary trial as protests erupted across the country over the brutal rape and murder of an eight-year-old girl child whose body parts were recently found in the capital’s Mirpur area....

item.title: Lengthy judicial process, weak probe obstacle to justice for rape
item.description: Human rights groups on Thursday condemned the recent surge in incidents of violence against children, including rape and murder, and blamed the lengthy judicial process and weak investigations as major obstacles to ensuring justice for victim children....

item.title: Opposition parties say SC secretariat abolition attack on judiciary
item.description: Differentpoliticalparties on Thursday kept opposing the abolition of the Supreme Court secretariat by the Bangladesh National Party-led government, saying that it would hamper judicial independence. ...Politics

item.title: AD to hear govt appeal against HC verdict on SC authority June 9
item.description: The government on Thursday filed an appeal seeking to overturn the High Court verdict that restored the Supreme Court’s authority over the control and discipline of the subordinate judges....

item.title: West Bengal hands over 75 acres to BSF for border fencing
item.description: India’s West Bengal government on Wednesday handed over an initial 75 acres of land to BSF to cover fencing of 27 km of the international border between India and Bangladesh, reported The Times of India on Thursday....

item.title: Public interest in savings certificates continues to fall
item.description: Net sales of national savings certificates (NSCs) remained in negative territory during the first nine months of the current financial year through March, indicating that the instruments are losing appeal among the savers......

item.title: Cabinet approves defence deal with Japan
item.description: The cabinet on Thursday approved a proposal to ratify the agreement titled Transfer of Defence Equipment and Technology, signed between Japan and Bangladesh....

item.title: Low ADP execution, weak pvt credit deepen economic risks: experts
item.description: Economists and business leaders on Thursday warned that weak implementation of development projects, falling private sector credit growth and continued dependence on bank borrowing by the government were intensifying pressure on Bangladesh’s economy, calling for structural reforms ahead of the budget for the financial year 2026-27......

item.title: Robi’s rejoinder, our reply
item.description: Robi Axiata PLC, in a rejoinder issued on Wednesday over a New Age report titled ‘Robi’s hiring of foreign executive challenged’ published on May 20, 2026, claimed that thenewspaperhad presented a misleading and defamatory narrative by omitting major portions of the company’s official response and relevant context......Newspapers

item.title: Wastes choke Dhaka city canals
item.description: Thirty-eight-year-old Mohammad Ali Akbar recalls swimming and catching fish in his childhood in Jirani Khal, an approximately four-kilometre-long canal flowing through Dhaka’s Khilgaon, Nandipara and Trimohoni areas. But now he finds the canal......

item.title: Bangladesh SOEs cost treasury Tk 88,200cr: WB
item.description: Bangladesh’s state-owned enterprises (SOEs) drained nearly Tk 88,200 crore from the national exchequer in a single year, emerging as one of the country’s biggest fiscal risks, according to a World Bank study......

item.title: Govt mulls removal of SIM tax, replacement charge
item.description: The government is considering reducing the tax on new SIM card issuance and abolishing taxes on SIM replacements and Internet of Things (IoT)/Machine-to-Machine (M2M) SIMs as part of efforts to expand the telecommunications sector and strengthen digital connectivity......

item.title: BB sets clearing house schedule for May 25, 26
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank on Thursday issued a revised schedule for the Bangladesh Automated Clearing House (BACH) and Real Time Gross Settlement (RTGS) systems for May 25 and 26, to facilitate financial transactions and trade activities ahead of the holy Eid-ul-Azha......City & Local Guides

item.title: Khulna road blocked demanding jute export
item.description: Jute workers in Khulna blocked road for the third consecutive day on Thursday, demanding the unconditional withdrawal of the ban on raw jute exports and compensation for workers who lost their jobs due to the suspension of exports......

item.title: BSEC holds workshop for woman investors
item.description: The Bangladesh Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday organised a free primary investment education workshop for women investors as part of its ongoing initiative to ensure effective participation of women in the capital market......

item.title: Govt takes on reforms to set up humane economy, says Khasru
item.description: Finance minister Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury on Thursday said that the government had undertaken an all-out reform programme to establish a welfare-oriented state and a humane economy for the country’s common people by moving away from an oligarchic and patronage-based economic system......

item.title: Oil gains, European stocks down on uncertain ME peace prospects
item.description: Oil prices jumped, European stock markets retreated and the dollar firmed Thursday as hopes of a Middle East peace accord faded on conflicting headlines on the state of talks......

item.title: Campuses need functional anti-harassment safeguards
item.description: THE committees instituted in schools, colleges and madrassahs to prevent sexual harassment are reported to have largely been non-functional because of the negligence of the authorities concerned, lack of coordination among agencies, under-reporting and social taboo. The High Court on May 14, 2009 directed educational institutions to institute the sexual violence......

item.title: Record private sector credit decline alarming
item.description: A RECORD collapse in private-sector credit growth exposes the alarming fragility of the economy and the failure to restore business confidence. Bangladesh Bank data show that private-sector credit growth fell to an unprecedented 4.72 per cent in March, the lowest since the central bank began compiling data in 2003. The decline follows a prolonged downward trend, from......Press Release Distribution

item.title: Moral crisis in health care
item.description: MORE than 480 children have died and thousands more have fallen ill in Bangladesh’s measles outbreak this year. At the same time, the president has been travelling abroad for medical consultations. These are not unrelated events. Read side by side, they expose the deepest fault line in Bangladesh’s public health system: one pathway for those who can purchase safety......

item.title: Invisible burden behind motherhood
item.description: ‘PARENTING’ has been a familiar term for generations, but in urban Bangladesh today, it has become more visible, competitive, and increasingly exhausting than before. Modern parenting now demands an almost impossible standard of perfection, from carefully curated lunchboxes to constant emotional availability and academic monitoring. Yet behind this......

item.title: Bridging education-employment gap
item.description: EDUCATION is the most powerful weapon for personal development and the development of the nation. It is the key to doors to knowledge, opportunity and social mobility. Yet in many developing countries such as Bangladesh, a widening gap between what students are taught in schools and what the job market requires is widening. This mismatch is now one of the......Bangladeshi Culture Course

item.title: Tax violence epidemic
item.description: IN BANGLADESH, taxation is increasingly beginning to resemble an elaborate hostage situation. The tax authorities make an allegation, name a number large enough to induce a stroke and then politely inform the taxpayer that, if they wish to challenge the allegation, they must first deposit a small fortune. Some may wonder whether the objective is revenue collection or competitive extortion......

item.title: SOS Children’s Villages, BSRM sign MoU to support vulnerable children
item.description: SOS Children’s Villages Bangladesh and BSRM Steels Ltd have launched a partnership to strengthen family-based alternative care services for vulnerable, orphaned and abandoned children in the country....Bangladeshi Culture Course

item.title: 2 Juba dal activists shot inside BNP office in Khulna
item.description: Two activists of Juba Dal, an associate body of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party, were shot inside a BNP office in Khulna city on Thursday....

item.title: Teen gang leader Ahmed arrested at Mohammadpur
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion has arrested an alleged teen gang leader with a pistol and 10 rounds of bullets from the Chand Udyan area of ​​Mohammadpur in the capital....

item.title: Train derails in Jhenaidah, services disrupted for 5hrs
item.description: A freight train wagon derailed in Kotchandpur upazila of Jhenaidah district in the early hours of Thursday, causing rail communication between Khulna and Rajshahi, along with the northern part of the country, suspended for around five hours....

item.title: CEC seeks political parties’ support to hold violence-free local govt polls
item.description: Chief election commissioner AMM Nasir Uddin on Thursday said that the Election Commission wanted to hold ‘bloodshed-free’ local government elections and would launch awareness campaigns to prevent violence during the polls....

item.title: Youth killed in Jashore
item.description: The police recovered the body of Abu Huraira Meem, 26, from a place near Jashore-Benapole Highway at Kitripur village of Jhikargachha upazila on Thursday morning....

item.title: SSC examinee dies from electrocution in Sunamganj
item.description: A Secondary School Certificate examinee died after being electrocuted on Thursday while working in an under-construction building at Tahirpur upazila in Sunamganj....

item.title: Inclusive budget, equal social protection demanded
item.description: Persons with disabilities on Thursday demanded an inclusive and rights-based national budget for the fiscal year 2026–27 with increased financial support, employment opportunities, and improved accessibility....

item.title: ‘Unified editorial platform’ urged
item.description: A group of editors and senior journalists on Thursday called for forming ‘an independent and unified editorial platform’ in Bangladesh to strengthen press freedom and ensure greater editorial independence....

item.title: Israeli strike damages hospital in south Lebanon
item.description: Lebanon said an Israeli strike on Thursday damaged a hospital in the country’s south, where Israel and Hezbollah have been trading fire despite a ceasefire....Press Release Distribution

item.title: Harvard announces reform to fight grade inflation
item.description: Harvard announced Wednesday it will limit how many top marks professors can give undergraduate students, moving to fight grade inflation at one of America’s most storied universities....

item.title: Ukraine boosts border security with Belarus over attack fears
item.description: Ukraine on Thursday said it is ramping up security in regions bordering Belarus after warning for weeks of a possible fresh attack launched from the territory of Russia’s chief regional ally....

item.title: Man lynched on suspicion of transformer theft in Jhalakati
item.description: A man was beaten to death at Kanthalia in Jhalakati on suspicion of transformer theft....

item.title: Experts fear vaccine gaps could worsen antimicrobial resistance
item.description: A new policy brief released by the Global Antibiotic Resistance Partnership has warned that widening immunisation gaps in Bangladesh could worsen antimicrobial resistance and reverse decades of public health progress....Bangladeshi Culture Course

item.title: IU teachers’ arrears reach Tk 10cr amid payment delays
item.description: The Islamic University is facing growing administrative and financial pressure as outstanding payments to teachers for examination-related duties have accumulated to nearly Tk 10 crore, with faculty members alleging irregular disbursement and systemic delays......

item.title: Conclave stresses stronger collaboration in food fortification
item.description: The Millers for Nutrition coalition, powered by TechnoServe, hosted the Bangladesh Country Conclave 2026 in Dhaka on Thursday, bringing together government officials, industry leaders, academics and development partners to strengthen......

item.title: Bangladesh Army Firing Competition ends
item.description: The closing and prize-giving ceremony of the Bangladesh Army Firing Competition-2026 was held at Cumilla Cantonment on Thursday....

item.title: Texas University professor visits DU, discusses cooperation
item.description: Professor Juan E Gonzalez of the University of Texas paid a courtesy call on University of Dhaka pro-vice-chancellor for administration Professor Mohammed Almujaddade Alfasane on Thursday, where both sides discussed expanding joint education and research activities....City & Local Guides

item.title: Digital access for people with disabilites govt’s priority: ICT minister
item.description: Posts, telecommunications and information technology minister Faqir Mahbub Anam on Thursday said that ensuring equal access to digital services for persons with disabilities was one of the government’s key priorities....

item.title: Health sector reform commission meets minister
item.description: Health Sector Reform Commission members, led by its chief Professor AK Azad Khan met health and family welfare minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Hossain on Thursday....

item.title: Justice, reparations, guarantee of non-recurrence demanded
item.description: Rights activists, survivors of enforced disappearance and family members of the victims on Thursday demanded justice and reparations for the survivors and victims’ families, as well as guarantees of non-recurrence....

item.title: Seminar, Gambhira held to raise awareness about RNPP
item.description: A seminar and a traditional Gambhira performance were organised at Pakshi Railway College to raise public awareness about the Rooppur Nuclear Power Plant and promote understanding of the peaceful use of nuclear energy....

item.title: Workshop urges urgent Sundarban conservation
item.description: An inception workshop of the Conservation and Restoration Initiatives in Sundarban Region Project was held in Dhaka on Wednesday, bringing together government officials, development partners, conservation experts and civil society......
